canonical_question: What is cultural metallurgy — mixing operational models for organizational resilience?
aliases: mixing operational models like alloying metals
constraints: Requires leadership that can tolerate managed ambiguity — organizations with strong command-and-control cultures will resist this framework
Not applicable during acute organizational crisis — tension management requires baseline stability to function
Paradox Theory is descriptive, not prescriptive — it identifies productive tensions but does not specify optimal tension levels per context
Mixing operational models creates coordination overhead — organizations under 100 employees may find the complexity cost exceeds the resilience benefit
Empirical evidence is primarily from large multinational firms — applicability to startups and SMEs is less well-documented

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs to diagnose existing structural defects, not design resilient structures
User is dealing with change resistance, not structure design
User needs a specific team resilience framework, not organization-wide cultural design
